options.label: A
options.text: Weight is the amount of matter in an object and never changes

options.label: B
options.text: Weight is the gravitational force pulling an object downwards and is measured in newtons

options.label: C
options.text: Weight is the same as mass and is measured in kilograms

options.label: D
options.text: Weight only applies to objects falling through the air

explanation: Weight is the gravitational force acting on an object and is measured in newtons (N). This is different from mass, which is the amount of matter in an object measured in kilograms. Weight can change depending on gravitational field strength, but mass does not.
